conbody.p: The following table shows the major differences between MACVLAN and IPVLAN:

entry.p: Uses MAC address for each MACVLAN device.
Note that, if a switch reaches the maximum number of MAC addresses it can store in its MAC table, connectivity can be lost.
Uses a single MAC address which does not limit the number of IPVLAN devices.
Netfilter rules for a global namespace cannot affect traffic to or from a MACVLAN device in a child namespace.
It is possible to control traffic to or from an IPVLAN device in L3 mode and L3S mode.

conbody.p: Both IPVLAN and MACVLAN do not require any level of encapsulation.
